name: DataGrid
description: Enterprise grid: sort, filter, inline edit, multi-level group (client + server), pin, virtualize, export.

api.name: DataGrid
api.description: Enterprise grid: sort, filter, inline edit, multi-level group (client + server), pin, virtualize, export.

parameters.name: Compact
parameters.description: Dense layout: header and body cells drop from px-3 py-2 to px-2 py-1 (and the root text shrinks to text-sm), so the effective row height genuinely decreases — not just the font size. The flag flows to every cell through DataGridContext{TItem}, so toggling it after first render re-pads the grid live. When virtualization is active (see VirtualItemSize) the default row-height estimate is auto-tightened under Compact unless you pass an explicit VirtualItemSize. Default false.

parameters.name: PageSizeOptions
parameters.description: The list of page-size options shown in the rows-per-page selector. Defaults to { 10, 25, 50, 100 }. Set to e.g. { 10, 50, 100, 200, 300 } for larger datasets, or to an empty array to hide the selector entirely.

parameters.name: ShowSearch
parameters.description: Show the global search box in the toolbar. Default true.

parameters.name: ShowColumnChooser
parameters.description: Show the Columns button (visibility/reorder menu). Default true.

parameters.name: ShowExport
parameters.description: Show the Export button and its format dropdown. Default true. Set false to hide export entirely. For fine-grained per-format control see ExportFormats.

parameters.name: ExportFormats
parameters.description: Which export formats appear in the Export dropdown. Default All. Set to e.g. Csv | Excel to hide PDF in WebAssembly where QuestPDF isn't supported. When no flags are set (or ShowExport is false), the Export button is hidden.

parameters.name: VirtualizeThreshold
parameters.description: Item count above which Blazor's <Virtualize> kicks in. Only relevant when ShowPagination is false (or PageSize > this threshold) — paged grids show at most PageSize rows at once and never trigger virtualization. Default 500.

parameters.name: VirtualItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated row height in CSS pixels — used by <Virtualize> to size the scroll spacers. Tune if your rows are taller (dense badges, two-line cells). Default 41px. When Compact is on and this parameter is left at its default, the estimate auto-tightens to CompactVirtualItemSizepx so the scroll spacers match the shorter compact rows; pass an explicit value to override either way.

parameters.name: VirtualOverscanCount
parameters.description: Extra rows rendered above and below the viewport to hide pop-in during fast scrolling. Default 3.

parameters.name: GroupByFields
parameters.description: Ordered list of fields for multi-level grouping. When set (non-empty) this takes precedence over the single GroupBy parameter. Each entry must be a column Field. The grid renders nested group rows with increasing indentation per level; per-level aggregates are shown on each group header row.

parameters.name: ShowGroupPanel
parameters.description: Shows a slim bar above the grid that lists the current grouping levels as removable chips, plus a dropdown to add a new level from any Groupable column. Default false.

parameters.name: GroupPanelText
parameters.description: Placeholder/hint text shown in the group panel when no fields are grouped.

parameters.name: ChildItemsSelector
parameters.description: When set, the grid renders a hierarchical tree-grid: this selector returns a row's child rows (null/empty = leaf). Each parent row gets an expand chevron in the tree column (see TreeColumnField). Distinct from DetailTemplate, which is a flat expand-for-detail fragment. Tree-grid mode and grouping are mutually exclusive — when both are configured, tree-grid mode wins.

parameters.name: TreeGridDefaultExpanded
parameters.description: Whether tree nodes start expanded. Default false.

parameters.name: TreeColumnField
parameters.description: Field of the column that carries the tree chevron + indentation. Null (default) uses the first visible column.

parameters.name: OnTreeNodeExpand
parameters.description: Raised when a tree node is expanded or collapsed (the item, plus its new expanded state).

parameters.name: Expandable
parameters.description: When true, adds a small Expand icon to the toolbar. Clicking it renders the grid in a fullscreen modal overlay. Default false.

parameters.name: IsExpanded
parameters.description: When true, the grid is currently rendered in fullscreen. Bind this for programmatic control, or leave unbound to let the grid manage its own state via the toolbar button.

parameters.name: FullscreenTitle
parameters.description: Optional title shown in the fullscreen header bar. Defaults to "Data Grid".

parameters.name: Virtualized
parameters.description: Enables Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.Virtualization.Virtualize{TItem} with an ItemsProvider — rows are fetched from the backend in scroll-driven windows instead of paged. Requires OnRangeRequest. When active, pagination is hidden automatically and the grid renders a single tall scrollable body.

parameters.name: OnRangeRequest
parameters.description: Range-fetch callback used in virtualised server mode. The grid invokes this whenever the viewport scrolls into an unloaded window. The callback must return the requested slice plus the current total row count so the scroll spacers stay accurate.

parameters.name: OnBatchSave
parameters.description: Fired when the user clicks "Save all" while EditMode is Batch. Args carry the buffered modifications + added rows. On a clean callback return (no exception), the grid's pending-changes buffer is cleared automatically.

parameters.name: ShowAddRow
parameters.description: When true and NewItemFactory is set, renders a thin "+ Add row" trigger below the body. Each click creates a TItem via the factory, pushes it into the added-rows buffer, and immediately shows it inline so cells can be filled in. Only meaningful in Batch.

parameters.name: NewItemFactory
parameters.description: Factory used by the "+ Add row" trigger to produce a fresh TItem. Required when ShowAddRow is true. The returned item is stored in the added-rows buffer; the user fills its cells inline and the row is delivered via OnBatchSave.

parameters.name: ColumnVirtualize
parameters.description: Simplified column virtualization: caps the number of horizontally- rendered data columns at MaxVisibleColumns. Pinned columns always make the cut. Default false.

parameters.name: MaxVisibleColumns
parameters.description: Maximum columns rendered when ColumnVirtualize is true. Default 30.

parameters.name: BatchSaveAllText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "Save all" button. Override for localization.

parameters.name: BatchDiscardText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "Discard" button. Override for localization.

parameters.name: BatchAddRowText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "+ Add row" trigger. Override for localization.

parameters.name: OnColumnResize
parameters.description: Raised when a column is resized through the built-in UI — a pointer drag on the resize handle, a keyboard nudge (arrow keys on the focused handle), or a double-click auto-fit-to-content. The event args carry the column id, the committed pixel width (already clamped to Min/Max), and an AutoFit flag that is true only for the double-click auto-fit path. Column widths persist automatically with the rest of the layout when EnableLayoutPersistence is on; this callback is for consumers that also want to observe/store widths.

parameters.name: SelectionKeySelector
parameters.description: Optional key projector that identifies items across re-fetches. In server-mode where Items is a fresh instance per page-load, the default reference-based stale-selection cleanup wipes selections that survived a page. When this selector is set, the grid identifies stale-vs-still-selected rows by comparing keys instead of references — so a selection on page 1 stays applied after a page-2 fetch returns a fresh wrapper for the same logical row. Returning null from the selector for any item falls back to reference equality for that row.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: CultureInfo used for cell value formatting (dates/numbers) and exports. When null (default), falls back to CurrentCulture. Useful when your app sets a different culture than the UI locale — e.g. force de-DE formatting in an English UI.

events.name: OnTreeNodeExpand
events.description: Raised when a tree node is expanded or collapsed (the item, plus its new expanded state).

events.name: OnBatchSave
events.description: Fired when the user clicks "Save all" while EditMode is Batch. Args carry the buffered modifications + added rows. On a clean callback return (no exception), the grid's pending-changes buffer is cleared automatically.

events.name: OnColumnResize
events.description: Raised when a column is resized through the built-in UI — a pointer drag on the resize handle, a keyboard nudge (arrow keys on the focused handle), or a double-click auto-fit-to-content. The event args carry the column id, the committed pixel width (already clamped to Min/Max), and an AutoFit flag that is true only for the double-click auto-fit path. Column widths persist automatically with the rest of the layout when EnableLayoutPersistence is on; this callback is for consumers that also want to observe/store widths.

enums.name: DataGridExportFormat
enums.description: Flags enum controlling which export formats appear in the DataGrid{TItem} toolbar's Export dropdown. Defaults to All so existing grids keep listing every format. Set to a subset (e.g. Csv | Excel) to hide individual formats — useful in WebAssembly where QuestPDF's PDF export throws PlatformNotSupportedException.

examples.title: Basic DataGrid
examples.code:
<DataGrid TItem="Employee" Items="@employees" PageSize="5" ShowPagination="true">
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Name" Field="Name" Sortable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Department" Field="Department" Sortable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Salary" Field="Salary" Sortable="true" Format="C0" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Start Date" Field="StartDate" Format="yyyy-MM-dd" />
</DataGrid>

examples.title: Column Groups + Footer Aggregates
examples.code:
@* DataGridColumnGroup wraps a contiguous run of column defs under a labelled parent
   <th colspan>. Set Aggregate on a column to surface a sticky footer total; FooterFormat
   formats just the total (cells in C0, totals in N0). *@
<DataGrid TItem="Employee" Items="@employees">
    <DataGridColumnGroup TItem="Employee" Label="Identity">
        <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Name" Field="Name" Sortable="true" />
        <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Department" Field="Department" Sortable="true" />
    </DataGridColumnGroup>
    <DataGridColumnGroup TItem="Employee" Label="Compensation">
        <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Salary" Field="Salary"
                           Sortable="true" Format="C0"
                           Aggregate="AggregateType.Sum" FooterFormat="N0" />
        <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Tenure" Field="StartDate"
                           Sortable="true" Format="yyyy-MM-dd" />
    </DataGridColumnGroup>
</DataGrid>

examples.title: Toolbar, Search & Filtering
examples.code:
<DataGrid TItem="Employee" Items="@employees" PageSize="5"
          ShowToolbar="true" ShowPagination="true">
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Name" Field="Name"
                       Sortable="true" Filterable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Salary" Field="Salary"
                       Filterable="true" FilterType="DataGridFilterType.Number" Format="C0" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Status" Field="Status"
                       Filterable="true" />
</DataGrid>

examples.title: Minimal toolbar (search only)
examples.code:
<DataGrid TItem="Employee" Items="@employees" PageSize="5"
          ShowToolbar="true"
          ShowColumnChooser="false"
          ShowExport="false">
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Name" Field="Name" Sortable="true" Filterable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Department" Field="Department" Sortable="true" Filterable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Salary" Field="Salary" Sortable="true" Format="C0" />
</DataGrid>

parameters.name: ServerVirtualizationProvider
parameters.description: When set, switches the body to ItemsProvider-mode virtualisation (window-fetched server scrolling). Null disables.

parameters.name: RowIndex
parameters.description: 0-based index of the owning row within DisplayedItems (non-grouped).

parameters.name: ColumnIndex
parameters.description: 0-based index of this column within the visible columns.

parameters.name: AriaColIndex
parameters.description: 1-based ARIA column index (includes any leading non-data columns).

parameters.name: Navigable
parameters.description: Whether this cell participates in roving-tabindex keyboard navigation. False in grouped mode where row indices are not globally unique.

parameters.name: FooterFormat
parameters.description: Optional .NET format string for the footer aggregate strip — overrides Format for the totals row only. Useful when the cell format differs from the desired totals format (e.g. cells "C2", totals "N0").

parameters.name: Visible
parameters.description: Two-way bindable column visibility. Seeds the registered column's initial visibility and lets a consumer show/hide the column programmatically via @bind-Visible. When the user toggles the column through the built-in column-chooser, VisibleChanged fires so the bound value follows. Default true.

parameters.name: VisibleChanged
parameters.description: Fires when the column's visibility changes through the built-in UI — the callback half of Visible's two-way binding.

events.name: VisibleChanged
events.description: Fires when the column's visibility changes through the built-in UI — the callback half of Visible's two-way binding.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Header label shown in the spanning th above the grouped columns.

parameters.name: HeaderClass
parameters.description: Extra classes for the spanning header cell — theming hook for the group.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.description: Nesting depth for multi-level grouping (0 = top level). Controls indentation.

parameters.name: Aggregates
parameters.description: Per-group aggregate strip rendered next to the group label.

parameters.name: AriaColIndex
parameters.description: 1-based ARIA column index (includes any leading non-data columns).

parameters.name: PageSizeOptions
parameters.description: Page-size options shown in the rows-per-page selector. Empty array hides the selector entirely.

parameters.name: RegisteredContent
parameters.description: The ToolbarContent{TItem} component the consumer placed inside the DataGrid, forwarded by the parent grid. When null, the toolbar's left slot renders just the search + filter chips; when non-null, its Class styles the wrapper and its ChildContent renders alongside the chips.

parameters.name: ShowSearch
parameters.description: Show the global search box. Default true.

parameters.name: ShowColumnChooser
parameters.description: Show the Columns (visibility/reorder) button. Default true.

parameters.name: ShowExport
parameters.description: Show the Export dropdown. Default true. Combined with ExportFormats.

parameters.name: ExportFormats
parameters.description: Which export formats to include in the dropdown. Default All.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Custom CSS classes applied to the wrapper around the toolbar content (the flex row holding your custom buttons + the filter chips).

source.content:
@namespace Lumeo @typeparam TItem @attribute [CascadingTypeParameter(nameof(TItem))] @implements IAsyncDisposable @inject Lumeo.Services.Localization.ILumeoLocalizer L @inject Lumeo.Services.ToastService Toast @{ // Defensive: keep the roving-focus cell within the current data extents. // This is a pure clamp of two ints — it never triggers a re-render — so it's // safe to run during render and covers every path that mutates _displayedItems // (sort, filter, paging, grouping, external data changes). ClampFocusedCell(); var context = new DataGridContext( Columns: _visibleColumns, // _visibleColumns is itself the filtered cache (Visible == true) — we // expose it explicitly under VisibleColumns so descendants don't have // to re-filter via Columns.Where(c => c.Visible) every render. VisibleColumns: _visibleColumns, DisplayedItems: _displayedItems.AsReadOnly(), SelectedItems: _selectedItems.AsReadOnly(), Sorts: _sorts, Filters: _filters, GlobalSearch: _globalSearch, CurrentPage: _currentPage, PageSize: PageSize, TotalCount: EffectiveTotalCount, SelectionMode: SelectionMode, EditMode: EditMode, IsLoading: IsLoading || (_serverService?.IsLoading ?? false), RowReorderable: RowReorderable, RowReorderPointerActive: RowReorderPointerActive, RowContextMenuEnabled: RowContextMenu is not null, OnRowClick: OnRowClick, OnRowDoubleClick: OnRowDoubleClick, OnSort: EventCallback.Factory.Create(this, HandleSort), OnFilter: EventCallback.Factory.Create(this, HandleFilter), OnSelectionChanged: EventCallback.Factory.Create(this, HandleSelectionChanged), OnCellEdit: OnCellEdit, IsRowSelected: IsRowSelected, IsRowExpanded: IsRowExpanded, IsRowEditing: IsRowEditing, ToggleSelection: ToggleSelection, SelectAll: SelectAll, ClearSelection: ClearSelection, ToggleRowExpand: ToggleRowExpand, StartRowEdit: StartRowEdit, CancelRowEdit: CancelRowEdit, CommitRowEdit: CommitRowEditAsync, GetEditValue: GetEditValue, SetEditValue: SetEditValue, RowClass: RowClass, RowStyle: RowStyle, PinnedLeftOffsets: ComputePinnedLeftOffsets(), PinnedRightOffsets: ComputePinnedRightOffsets(), GroupedSections: _groupedSections.Count > 0 ? _groupedSections.AsReadOnly() : null, GroupBy: GroupBy, IsGrouped: !IsTreeGridMode && EffectiveGroupFields.Count == 1 && _groupedSections.Count > 0, IsGroupExpanded: IsGroupExpanded, ToggleGroupExpand: ToggleGroupExpand, Culture: EffectiveCulture, GroupFields: _runtimeGroupFields.AsReadOnly(), GroupTree: (!IsTreeGridMode && EffectiveGroupFields.Count > 1 && _groupedTree.Count > 0) ? _groupedTree.AsReadOnly() : null, IsGroupPathExpanded: IsGroupPathExpanded, ToggleGroupPath: ToggleGroupPath, AddGroupField: AddGroupField, RemoveGroupField: RemoveGroupField, ShowGroupPanel: ShowGroupPanel, ChildItemsSelector: ChildItemsSelector, IsTreeGrid: IsTreeGridMode, TreeColumnField: EffectiveTreeColumnField, IsTreeNodeExpanded: IsTreeNodeExpanded, ToggleTreeNode: it => _ = ToggleTreeNodeAsync(it), TreeLevelOf: TreeLevelOf, TreeHasChildren: TreeHasChildrenOf, // --- Batch-edit context fields — wired to the per-cell pending-changes // buffer (_batchChanges) and the additive new-row buffer (_batchAdded). IsBatchEdit: EditMode == DataGridEditMode.Batch, IsCellDirty: IsCellDirty, GetBatchValue: GetBatchValue, SetBatchValue: SetBatchValue, GridId: _gridId, FocusedRow: _focusedRow, FocusedCol: _focusedCol, RowCount: _displayedItems.Count, ColCount: _visibleColumns.Count, OnCellKeyDown: HandleCellKeyDownAsync, OnHeaderKeyDown: HandleHeaderKeyDownAsync, Striped: Striped, HasPinnedLeft: _orderedColumns.Any(c => c.Visible && c.Pin == PinDirection.Left), Hoverable: Hoverable, ColumnGroups: _columnGroups, Compact: Compact ); } @{ RenderFragment gridRoot = @
@* Declarative column definitions *@
@ChildContent
@if (_serverService?.Error is not null) {
@L["DataGrid.ErrorLoadingData", _serverService.Error ?? ""]
} @if (ShowToolbar) { } @if (_contextMenuVisible) {
@if (RowContextMenu is not null && _contextMenuItem is not null) { @RowContextMenu(_contextMenuItem) }
} @if (EditMode == DataGridEditMode.Batch && HasPendingChanges) { @* Batch-edit pending-changes strip. Uses warning/amber theme tokens from lumeo.css (--color-warning, --color-warning-text). Sits above the table body so it's visually associated with dirty cells. *@
@{ // _batchChanges contains entries for both modified existing rows AND // newly-added rows (the latter seeded with an empty dict). Count only // changes to *existing* rows by excluding items that are in _batchAdded. var modifiedCount = _batchChanges.Keys.Count(k => !_batchAdded.Contains(k)); var pendingTotal = modifiedCount + _batchAdded.Count; } @($"{pendingTotal} pending change(s)")
} @if (ShowGroupPanel) { @* rc.41: real drop zone — header DnD now lands here. Visual highlight via _isGroupPanelDragOver. The dropped column's field comes from _dragState.SourceColumnId (set by HeaderCell.HandleDragStart). *@
@if (_runtimeGroupFields.Count == 0) { @(GroupPanelText ?? L["DataGrid.GroupPanelPlaceholder"]) } else { @for (var gi = 0; gi < _runtimeGroupFields.Count; gi++) { var field = _runtimeGroupFields[gi]; var col = EffectiveColumns.FirstOrDefault(c => c.Field == field); var label = col?.Title ?? field; @* div (not span) wrapper: the Tooltip below renders a block
, which a cannot legally contain — the browser would auto-close the chip before the tooltip, ejecting the remove button outside the bordered chip. inline-flex keeps the inline chip layout on a div (Codex P3). *@
@label @L["DataGrid.RemoveGrouping"]
@if (gi < _runtimeGroupFields.Count - 1) { } } } @{ var addableCols = EffectiveColumns .Where(c => c.Groupable && c.Field is not null && !_runtimeGroupFields.Contains(c.Field!)) .ToList(); } @if (_runtimeGroupFields.Count > 0) { @* Clear-all button: lives next to the chips so it's discoverable and matches the per-chip X visual. rc.41 polish. *@ @L["DataGrid.ClearAllGrouping"] } @if (addableCols.Count > 0) { @* 2.2.0 — was a native 's // caret/typing keys (and its own Tab/Enter/Escape handling) untouched, since // those keydowns originate inside the editor input. private bool _navKeysPrevented; private static readonly Lumeo.Services.PreventDefaultKeyRule[] NavKeyRules = [ new("ArrowDown", SkipEditable: true), new("ArrowUp", SkipEditable: true), new("ArrowLeft", SkipEditable: true), new("ArrowRight", SkipEditable: true), new("Home", SkipEditable: true), new("End", SkipEditable: true), new("PageDown", SkipEditable: true), new("PageUp", SkipEditable: true), new(" ", SkipEditable: true), new("Spacebar", SkipEditable: true), ]; /// Clamp the roving-focus coordinates to the current data extents. /// Called after any data/sort/filter/page change so the stored cell never /// dangles past the visible range. private void ClampFocusedCell() { var colCount = _visibleColumns.Count; if (colCount == 0) { _focusedCol = 0; _focusedRow = -1; return; } if (_focusedCol < 0) _focusedCol = 0; if (_focusedCol >= colCount) _focusedCol = colCount - 1; var rowCount = _displayedItems.Count; if (_focusedRow < -1) _focusedRow = -1; if (_focusedRow >= rowCount) _focusedRow = rowCount - 1; // may land on -1 if no rows } private async Task MoveFocusAsync(int newRow, int newCol) { var colCount = _visibleColumns.Count; if (colCount == 0) return; newCol = Math.Clamp(newCol, 0, colCount - 1); var rowCount = _displayedItems.Count; // Allow -1 (header). Don't allow going past last body row. if (newRow < -1) newRow = -1; if (newRow > rowCount - 1) newRow = Math.Max(-1, rowCount - 1); if (newRow == _focusedRow && newCol == _focusedCol) { // Still re-focus the DOM element (e.g. Home pressed twice). await FocusCellAsync(newRow, newCol); return; } _focusedRow = newRow; _focusedCol = newCol; StateHasChanged(); await FocusCellAsync(newRow, newCol); } private async Task FocusCellAsync(int row, int col) { try { await Interop.FocusElement(CellElementId(row, col)); } catch (Microsoft.JSInterop.JSDisconnectedException) { /* circuit gone */ } catch { /* element may not be rendered (virtualized / grouped) — acceptable */ } } /// Keydown handler invoked from a body /// whose <td> currently has focus. internal async Task HandleCellKeyDownAsync(int row, int col, KeyboardEventArgs e) { // Keep our notion of the focused cell in sync with where the event came from. _focusedRow = row; _focusedCol = col; var colCount = _visibleColumns.Count; var rowCount = _displayedItems.Count; if (colCount == 0) return; switch (e.Key) { case "ArrowDown": await MoveFocusAsync(row + 1, col); break; case "ArrowUp": // From the first body row, jump up into the header row. await MoveFocusAsync(row <= 0 ? -1 : row - 1, col); break; case "ArrowRight": await MoveFocusAsync(row, col + 1); break; case "ArrowLeft": await MoveFocusAsync(row, col - 1); break; case "Home": if (e.CtrlKey) await MoveFocusAsync(0, 0); else await MoveFocusAsync(row, 0); break; case "End": if (e.CtrlKey) await MoveFocusAsync(rowCount - 1, colCount - 1); else await MoveFocusAsync(row, colCount - 1); break; case "PageDown": await MoveFocusAsync(row + KeyboardPageStep, col); break; case "PageUp": // Clamp to -1 (header band) instead of 0, so PageUp on row 0 // moves focus up to the column-header row instead of being a // no-op. MoveFocusAsync already understands row=-1 as the // header (line 684). await MoveFocusAsync(Math.Max(-1, row - KeyboardPageStep), col); break; case " ": case "Spacebar": if (SelectionMode != DataGridSelectionMode.None && row >= 0 && row < rowCount) { ToggleSelection(_displayedItems[row]); } break; case "a": case "A": if (e.CtrlKey && SelectionMode == DataGridSelectionMode.Multiple) { SelectAll(); } break; // Enter / F2 (enter edit mode) are handled inside DataGridCell itself // because that's where the per-cell edit state lives. } } /// Keydown handler invoked from a header . internal async Task HandleHeaderKeyDownAsync(int col, KeyboardEventArgs e) { _focusedRow = -1; _focusedCol = col; var colCount = _visibleColumns.Count; if (colCount == 0) return; switch (e.Key) { case "ArrowRight": await MoveFocusAsync(-1, col + 1); break; case "ArrowLeft": await MoveFocusAsync(-1, col - 1); break; case "ArrowDown": await MoveFocusAsync(0, col); break; case "Home": await MoveFocusAsync(-1, 0); break; case "End": await MoveFocusAsync(-1, colCount - 1); break; // Enter / Space → trigger sort is handled inside DataGridHeaderCell. } } private async ValueTask.IndexedItem>> ServerVirtualizationProviderImpl(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.Virtualization.ItemsProviderRequest req) { if (OnRangeRequest is null) { return new Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.Virtualization.ItemsProviderResult.IndexedItem>( Array.Empty.IndexedItem>(), 0); } var rangeRequest = new DataGridRangeRequest( req.StartIndex, req.Count, _sorts.Count > 0 ? new List(_sorts) : null, _filters.Count > 0 ? new List(_filters) : null, _globalSearch, req.CancellationToken); var resp = await OnRangeRequest(rangeRequest); var indexed = resp.Items .Select((it, i) => new DataGridBody.IndexedItem(it, req.StartIndex + i)) .ToList(); TotalCount = resp.TotalCount; return new Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.Virtualization.ItemsProviderResult.IndexedItem>( indexed, resp.TotalCount); } /// /// Forces the virtualised server view to discard its window cache and /// refetch from the current scroll position. Called automatically when /// sort/filter/search change; expose as @ref if you need to /// trigger it manually (e.g. after an external data mutation). /// public Task RefreshVirtualizedAsync() => _bodyRef is not null ? _bodyRef.RefreshVirtualizeAsync() : Task.CompletedTask; private List> _columnDefs = new(); private List> _orderedColumns = new(); // Cached visible-columns projection — recomputed only when columns change or // visibility is toggled, not on every Blazor render cycle. private System.Collections.ObjectModel.ReadOnlyCollection> _visibleColumns = new List>().AsReadOnly(); private void RefreshVisibleColumns() { // Pinned columns must be PHYSICALLY partitioned in the render/DOM order — // left-pinned first, unpinned in the middle, right-pinned last — because // `position: sticky` can only anchor a cell to a scroll-container edge; it // cannot relocate the cell across its siblings. A right-pinned column left // in its declared position (e.g. the leading column) would keep rendering // on the LEFT while carrying a `right:` offset that never visually engages — // the exact "pin right ends up on the left" defect. LINQ OrderBy is a stable // sort, so each pin group keeps its relative order; partitioning here — the // single chokepoint every pin path funnels through (column chooser, header // pin control, restored layouts, declared pins, drag-reorder) — makes the // DOM order match the sticky edge everywhere at once. Idempotent: an // already-partitioned order sorts to itself, so the frequent parameter-set // refreshes cost only a stable sort over the (small) column list. _orderedColumns = _orderedColumns .OrderBy(c => c.Pin switch { PinDirection.Left => 0, PinDirection.Right => 2, _ => 1 }) .ToList(); IEnumerable> q = _orderedColumns.Where(c => c.Visible); // Simplified column virtualization (rc.36): cap the rendered-column // count to keep wide grids cheap. Pinned columns always make the cut; // unpinned columns are taken in order until MaxVisibleColumns is hit. if (ColumnVirtualize && MaxVisibleColumns > 0) { var all = q.ToList(); if (all.Count > MaxVisibleColumns) { var pinned = all.Where(c => c.Pin != PinDirection.None).ToList(); var unpinned = all.Where(c => c.Pin == PinDirection.None).ToList(); var roomForUnpinned = Math.Max(0, MaxVisibleColumns - pinned.Count); q = pinned.Concat(unpinned.Take(roomForUnpinned)); } else { q = all; } } _visibleColumns = q.ToList().AsReadOnly(); } private List _processedItems = new(); private List _displayedItems = new(); // The raw, unfiltered server page (ServerMode only) — set ONLY when Items is actually // reassigned (a genuine server refresh), NEVER by a local expand/collapse toggle. // RegroupServerItems() must regroup from THIS, not from _displayedItems: _displayedItems // is the expand-filtered OUTPUT of the last regroup, so reading it back as the INPUT to // the next regroup means a local toggle regroups an ever-shrinking subset — collapsing // group A removes A's rows from _displayedItems, so collapsing a second group B then // regroups from a list that no longer contains A's rows at all, making A's group ROW // vanish entirely (not just its children). With GroupsExpandedByDefault=false the very // first expand click sees _displayedItems already empty and falls into the "no items" // branch, wiping grouping outright — a full empty-state subtree swap that looks like the // whole grid reloaded. # DataGrid

Enterprise grid: sort, filter, inline edit, multi-level group (client + server), pin, virtualize, export.

**Package:** `Lumeo.DataGrid` · **Category:** Data Display/Tables
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/data-grid

The flag flows to every cell through DataGridContext{TItem}, so toggling it after first render re-pads the grid live. When virtualization is active (see VirtualItemSize) the default row-height estimate is auto-tightened under Compact unless you pass an explicit VirtualItemSize. Default false. |

## Events
- `OnTreeNodeExpand` (`EventCallback<TItem>`) — Raised when a tree node is expanded or collapsed (the item, plus its new expanded state).
- `IsExpandedChanged` (`EventCallback<bool>`)
- `OnLayoutSave` (`EventCallback<DataGridLayout>`)
- `OnSaveNamedLayout` (`EventCallback<DataGridNamedLayout>`)
- `OnDeleteNamedLayout` (`EventCallback<string>`)
- `OnServerRequest` (`EventCallback<DataGridServerRequest>`)
- `OnRowClick` (`EventCallback<TItem>`)
- `OnRowDoubleClick` (`EventCallback<TItem>`)
- `OnCellEdit` (`EventCallback<CellEditEventArgs<TItem>>`)
- `OnRowEdit` (`EventCallback<RowEditEventArgs<TItem>>`)
- `OnBatchSave` (`EventCallback<DataGridBatchSaveEventArgs<TItem>>`) — Fired when the user clicks "Save all" while EditMode is Batch. Args carry the buffered modifications + added rows. On a clean callback return (no exception), the grid's pending-changes buffer is cleared automatically.
- `OnColumnReorder` (`EventCallback<ColumnReorderEventArgs>`)
- `OnColumnResize` (`EventCallback<ColumnResizeEventArgs>`) — Raised when a column is resized through the built-in UI — a pointer drag on the resize handle, a keyboard nudge (arrow keys on the focused handle), or a double-click auto-fit-to-content. The event args carry the column id, the committed pixel width (already clamped to Min/Max), and an AutoFit flag that is true only for the double-click auto-fit path. Column widths persist automatically with the rest of the layout when EnableLayoutPersistence is on; this callback is for consumers that also want to observe/store widths.
- `OnRowReorder` (`EventCallback<RowReorderEventArgs<TItem>>`)
- `OnError` (`EventCallback<Exception>`)
- `SelectedItemsChanged` (`EventCallback<IReadOnlyList<TItem>>`)
